meta.heroImageAlt: JackiePortfolio homepage with hero section, navigation, and portfolio entry points.

hero.title: JackiePortfolio
hero.subtitle: Responsive portfolio website for a creative professional

items.label: Services
items.value: UX design, frontend development, responsive design, SEO setup, contact form integration

items.label: Technology
items.value: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Resend

challenge.title: The challenge
challenge.intro: The client needed a polished online presence that could support real creative work—not just look good in a mockup.
points: Present video and campaign work clearly
Make portfolio browsing easier for visitors
Support professional credibility with a cohesive layout
Work well across mobile and desktop
Provide a clear way for visitors to get in touch
Support future content updates without rebuilding the UI each time

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.intro: Before writing large blocks of new code, the focus was on structure, content, and how people would actually use the site.
points: Start with the client’s content and goals
Build a structure that makes work easy to browse
Prioritize responsive layouts from the beginning
Keep the gallery media experience central
Use reusable components instead of one-off layouts
Improve details gradually through iteration
Build with maintainability in mind for future updates

uxDecisions.title: UX and design decisions
uxDecisions.intro: The site is media-heavy, so usability and clarity mattered more than decorative effects.

items.title: Gallery-first experience
items.description: Visual work leads the page so visitors can scan campaigns and video projects quickly.

items.title: Larger media previews
items.description: Videos and stills open in a more immersive enlarge view with responsive sizing caps.

items.title: Expandable project details
items.description: Gallery cards include collapsible detail sections so users can learn more without leaving the gallery.

items.title: Responsive spacing refinements
items.description: Padding, breakpoints, and layout spacing were adjusted through focused commits rather than large risky rewrites.

items.title: Mobile navigation improvements
items.description: Bottom navigation and dialog behavior were refined for smaller screens and clearer access.

items.title: Back-to-top for long pages
items.description: A back-to-top control helps visitors return to the top after browsing a long portfolio page.

items.title: Accessible carousel controls
items.description: The Creative Council carousel uses scroll snapping with labeled next and previous controls.

items.title: Purposeful micro-interactions
items.description: Hover states and small motion support usability without overwhelming the content.

technical.title: Technical implementation
technical.intro: The build uses a modern frontend stack with a content structure that stays manageable as the portfolio grows.
points: Built with Next.js, React, and TypeScript
Styled with Tailwind CSS
Centralized gallery metadata in a typed data file
Added gallery video entries and poster metadata for campaigns
Built reusable UI components across gallery, journey, and contact areas
Added CreativeCouncilCarousel with scroll snapping and accessible next/previous controls
Added GalleryCardDetailsFooter with expandable details and masonry relayout support
Implemented larger gallery media dialogs for images and videos
Used responsive CSS with modern viewport units to improve media scaling on different devices
Implemented a contact API route with Resend for production email delivery
Added environment variable checks so missing email config fails safely in development
Added input sanitization for contact form content
Added graceful fallback behavior when email configuration is not set
Updated resume PDF configuration and journey/resume content structure

seo.title: SEO, discoverability, and trust
seo.intro: The goal was a solid foundation—not inflated claims about rankings or traffic.
points: Semantic page structure with clear headings
Descriptive project and section content
SEO-friendly metadata on key pages
Responsive design that works across common devices
Fast, maintainable frontend structure
Content organized so visitors and AI search systems can understand what the site offers
Honest case study language without fake performance metrics

pairs.challenge: The portfolio relied heavily on visual media, so the gallery needed to feel immersive without breaking on smaller screens.
pairs.solution: Media sizing was refined with responsive CSS, viewport-based caps, and improved enlarge dialog behavior.

pairs.challenge: The contact form needed to be production-ready but safe during development.
pairs.solution: The API route checks for required Resend environment variables and returns a safe placeholder response when configuration is missing.

pairs.challenge: Gallery content needed to be updated without rewriting layout code.
pairs.solution: Media data was centralized in a typed gallery file so new campaigns and metadata could be added cleanly.

pairs.challenge: Small spacing and mobile issues affected polish across the site.
pairs.solution: The site was refined through many focused commits instead of risky large changes.

outcome.title: Outcome
outcome.intro: This project delivered practical improvements the client can build on—without inventing traffic or revenue numbers.
points: A more polished professional portfolio experience
Stronger gallery browsing experience for video and campaign work
Improved mobile usability across navigation and media views
Clearer contact flow for inquiries
Maintainable content structure for future portfolio updates
More production-ready email handling with safe development fallbacks
Better foundation for SEO and AI-search discoverability
A project that demonstrates design thinking, frontend development, and client-focused problem solving

lessons.title: Lessons learned
points: Real client projects require iteration—not one perfect launch
Small UX details add up to a more trustworthy experience
Media-heavy websites need careful responsive testing
Contact forms need safe production handling and clear developer fallbacks
Case studies should explain decisions, not just show screenshots
Maintainable structure matters even for smaller client websites

cta.title: Need a website that does more than look good?
cta.description: I build websites that are designed around your goals, your customers, and the way people actually use the web.
